topic.learning_goal: Learn to describe your current self through mindset, habits, and direction rather than labels alone.

angles.title: State the time frame
content: Tell the listener what period of life you are reflecting on.
That makes change and continuity easier to follow.

angles.title: Describe the change
content: Say what has actually changed in habits, mindset, or priorities.
Use contrast to make the shift visible.

angles.title: Explain what shaped it
content: Mention the experience, decision, or pressure that influenced the change.
That gives the reflection a real cause.

angles.title: End with your current direction
content: Finish with who you are now or where you think you are heading.
Leave room for uncertainty when that sounds more honest.

sequence.target: State the period you are reflecting on and the biggest shift.
Explain what drove the change and how it unfolded.
End with your present priorities, identity, or future direction.

high_score_tips: Reflection sounds stronger when you compare then and now clearly.
Name one cause of change instead of using vague personal growth language.
A modest ending often sounds more mature than a perfectly confident future plan.
If the future is uncertain, say that directly and calmly.

content.problem: Talking only in general growth language
content.why_it_hurts: The answer feels vague if there is no visible contrast between the past and now.
content.fix: Use one habit, decision, or value that changed clearly over time.

content.why_it_hurts: Reflection is weaker when the listener cannot see what shaped it.
content.fix: Mention the pressure, event, or realisation behind the shift.

content.why_it_hurts: That can sound less honest than a realistic, open-ended answer.
content.fix: Allow room for change if that matches your real situation.

content.why_it_hurts: The response sounds memorised if it stays at the level of general ideas and never lands on one lived detail.
content.fix: Add one concrete moment, reaction, or small consequence that actually happened to you.

content.problem: Reflection that only repeats the opening
content.why_it_hurts: The ending feels flat if it only restates the topic instead of adding insight or change.
content.fix: Use the final lines to show what changed, what you realised, or how you would respond now.

language.wrong_example: I become more better at handling pressure.
language.better_example: I have become better at handling pressure.
language.reason: Use the present perfect for growth that continues until now, and do not double the comparative.

language.wrong_example: In future I will focus more on balance.
language.better_example: In the future, I will focus more on balance.
language.reason: The article makes the phrase more natural in standard spoken English.

language.problem: Overusing very or really with basic adjectives
language.wrong_example: It was very good and really important for me.
language.better_example: It turned out to be more meaningful than I expected.
language.reason: A precise evaluation sounds more natural than stacking intensifiers on basic adjectives.

language.problem: Repeating I think in every sentence
language.wrong_example: I think it is useful, and I think it matters because I think it affects daily life.
language.better_example: What makes it useful to me is the way it affects daily life.
language.reason: Removing repeated I think makes the answer sound more confident and more fluent.

core.example: I would naturally use "change" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "growth" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "priority" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "direction" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "reflection" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "identity" when talking about this topic.

extended.note: Useful when reflecting on change over time.
Good for present identity and future answers.
Useful for growth and long-term reflection.

core.example: I would use "over the past few years" naturally in this answer.
I would use "I have become more" naturally in this answer.
I would use "these days" naturally in this answer.
I would use "I am still figuring out" naturally in this answer.

core.phrase: looking back, I can see that
core.example: I would use "looking back, I can see that" naturally in this answer.

core.phrase: what matters to me now is
core.example: I would use "what matters to me now is" naturally in this answer.

extended.example: I would use "the biggest shift has been" when making the answer sound more natural.

extended.phrase: I no longer feel the need to
extended.example: I would use "I no longer feel the need to" when making the answer sound more natural.

extended.phrase: at this point in my life
extended.example: I would use "at this point in my life" when making the answer sound more natural.

extended.phrase: I want to leave room for change
extended.example: I would use "I want to leave room for change" when making the answer sound more natural.

framework.goal: Reflect on change through contrast, cause, and current direction.

moves: State the time frame and the main contrast.
Show the listener what has changed.
Explain the cause or turning point behind the change.
Use one example that shows the shift in a concrete way.
End with your current priorities or future direction.
If needed, leave room for uncertainty.

delivery_markers: At this point in my life, ...

samples.answer:
The best way i can describe who i am at this point in life is something I can talk about quite naturally. If I had to describe who I am now, I would say I am more grounded than I used to be, but still quite ambitious in a quiet way. I am less interested in proving things quickly and more interested in building something stable, whether that means work, habits, or relationships. At the same time, I do not feel fully finished or fully defined, which I think is normal. There are still areas where I am changing, but I have a better sense of what matters to me and what does not.

What made it worth talking about was not only the event or idea itself, but also the effect it had on the way I think or act. So the person I am now feels more consistent than before, even if the full picture is still evolving. Overall, it is a topic that feels real to me because it connects to ordinary life rather than a perfect story.
highlights: Band 6 answer matched to the 描述现在的自己 topic
Keeps spoken rhythm while staying specific and teachable

opening: The topic that comes to mind is the best way I can describe who I am at this point in life.
I would first explain the background clearly.
body: Then I would add the central detail or turning point.
After that, I would show the result or practical effect.
closing: Finally, I would end with the takeaway or present view.

upgrades.improved: So the person I am now feels more consistent than before, even if the full picture is still evolving.
upgrades.why: A specific reason sounds much more natural than a general claim.
upgrades.note: Use the personal effect to close the answer.

upgrades.improved: At the same time, I do not feel fully finished or fully defined, which I think is normal.
upgrades.why: A clear turning point gives the answer shape and movement.
upgrades.note: This is especially useful in spoken storytelling and reflective answers.

upgrades.original: It was very important to me.
upgrades.improved: What made it important was the effect it had on the way I handled similar situations later.
upgrades.why: This explains importance through a concrete effect instead of a vague adjective.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want a simple but natural reflective sentence.

upgrades.original: I learned a lot from it.
upgrades.improved: I came away from it with a clearer idea of what I would do next time.
upgrades.why: This sounds more spoken and more specific than a flat learned a lot.
upgrades.note: Good for endings that focus on takeaway and future behaviour.

samples.answer:
The topic that comes to mind for me is the best way I can describe who I am at this point in life. If I had to describe who I am now, I would say I am more grounded than I used to be, but still quite ambitious in a quiet way. I am less interested in proving things quickly and more interested in building something stable, whether that means work, habits, or relationships. What stood out most was that at the same time, I do not feel fully finished or fully defined, which I think is normal.

After that, there are still areas where I am changing, but I have a better sense of what matters to me and what does not. What I find interesting about it now is that the situation seemed fairly ordinary at the time, but it revealed something more lasting about how I handle pressure, choices, or relationships. So the person I am now feels more consistent than before, even if the full picture is still evolving. So if I were explaining this in a speaking test, I would focus less on sounding dramatic and more on why the experience or idea genuinely stayed with me.
highlights: Band 7 answer matched to the 描述现在的自己 topic
Keeps spoken rhythm while staying specific and teachable

band_guide.focus: Stronger reflection and clearer cause of change
opening: The topic that comes to mind is the best way I can describe who I am at this point in life.
I would first explain the background clearly.
body: Then I would add the central detail or turning point.
After that, I would show the result or practical effect.
closing: Finally, I would end with the takeaway or present view.

upgrades.improved: So the person I am now feels more consistent than before, even if the full picture is still evolving.
upgrades.why: A specific reason sounds much more natural than a general claim.
upgrades.note: Use the personal effect to close the answer.

upgrades.improved: At the same time, I do not feel fully finished or fully defined, which I think is normal.
upgrades.why: A clear turning point gives the answer shape and movement.
upgrades.note: This is especially useful in spoken storytelling and reflective answers.

upgrades.improved: What helped most was that it changed the way I judged the situation in practice.
upgrades.why: This sounds more mature and more natural than the vague phrase helped me a lot.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want to show growth without sounding dramatic.

upgrades.improved: It gave me a more balanced way to look at similar situations afterwards.
upgrades.why: Balanced way to look at is a more idiomatic reflective phrase in spoken English.
upgrades.note: Works well in opinion, process, and experience answers.

samples.answer:
If I had to answer this topic in a more reflective way, I would still begin with the best way I can describe who I am at this point in life. If I had to describe who I am now, I would say I am more grounded than I used to be, but still quite ambitious in a quiet way. I am less interested in proving things quickly and more interested in building something stable, whether that means work, habits, or relationships. The turning point, for me, was that at the same time, I do not feel fully finished or fully defined, which I think is normal.

From there, there are still areas where I am changing, but I have a better sense of what matters to me and what does not. What matters now is not the surface event or statement alone, but the shift in perspective behind it. So the person I am now feels more consistent than before, even if the full picture is still evolving. In that sense, the topic feels useful because it shows not only what happened or what I think, but also how my judgment has become a little more precise over time.

Keeps spoken rhythm while staying specific and teachable

opening: The topic that comes to mind is the best way I can describe who I am at this point in life.
I would first explain the background clearly.
body: Then I would add the central detail or turning point.
After that, I would show the result or practical effect.
closing: Finally, I would end with the takeaway or present view.

upgrades.improved: So the person I am now feels more consistent than before, even if the full picture is still evolving.
upgrades.why: A specific reason sounds much more natural than a general claim.
upgrades.note: Use the personal effect to close the answer.

upgrades.improved: At the same time, I do not feel fully finished or fully defined, which I think is normal.
upgrades.why: A clear turning point gives the answer shape and movement.
upgrades.note: This is especially useful in spoken storytelling and reflective answers.

upgrades.improved: What stayed with me was not the event itself, but the shift it created in my judgment afterwards.
upgrades.why: This upgrade sounds more nuanced and more reflective than a broad claim like it changed me.
upgrades.note: Strong for higher-band closings that need subtlety rather than drama.

upgrades.improved: Its real value lay in the way it quietly reshaped my response patterns over time.
upgrades.why: Quietly reshaped and over time make the reflection sound more precise and more natural.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want a sophisticated closing without sounding academic.

checklist: Compare the past and present clearly.
End with your current position or a realistic future direction.
self_prompts: How certain are you about the future?
